chief_complaint: A 22-year-old woman presented with CRAB pneumonia and sepsis after auxiliary liver transplantation.
history: The patient underwent orthotopic liver transplantation for acute liver failure. Postoperatively, she developed CRAB pneumonia and septicemia confirmed by imaging, bronchoscopy, and metagenomic next-generation sequencing. She received combination therapy with SUL-DUR, meropenem, eravacycline, and neutralized polymyxin B.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings not provided.
labs: Blood and sputum cultures confirmed CRAB susceptibility to SUL-DUR. Inflammatory markers (CRP, IL-6, PCT) and pathogen loads markedly decreased.
correct_diagnosis: Carbapenem-Resistant Acinetobacter baumannii (CRAB) pneumonia and sepsis
differentials: Carbapenem-Resistant Acinetobacter baumannii (CRAB) pneumonia and sepsis
Pneumonia due to other Gram-negative bacteria
Sepsis due to other bacterial infections
correct_ranking: Carbapenem-Resistant Acinetobacter baumannii (CRAB) pneumonia and sepsis
Pneumonia due to other Gram-negative bacteria
